title: Quiina cruegeriana
intro: Quiina cruegeriana is a plant species native to the tropical forests of South America, belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimalarial activities, as well as its potential to treat various infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Quiina cruegeriana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine, with preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts being made from its leaves and bark to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Quiina cruegeriana is a species of plant in the family Ochnaceae. It belongs to the genus Quiina, which comprises several species of flowering plants. Quiina cruegeriana is native to Central and South America, and its classification is further divided into various ranks. At the kingdom level, it falls under Plantae, at the phylum level under Magnoliophyta, and at the class level under Magnoliopsida. The specific classification of Quiina cruegeriana makes it a member of the Ochnaceae family within the order Ochnales.
